label: PASS
rationale: MBRControl was set Enable=true and Done=true with an empty DoneOnReset set. The evidence says an empty set does not change Done on any reset, so after PowerCycle Done remains true and LBA 0 is read from media pattern 99, not the MBR table.

label: FAIL
rationale: With Enable=true, Done=true, and DoneOnReset empty, PowerCycle must not clear Done. MBR shadowing is therefore inactive, so the expected LBA 0 data is the prior media pattern 99; returning CAFE1122 from the MBR table is not protocol-compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: DoneOnReset contained only PowerCycle, while the reset event was ProtocolStackReset. The packet distinguishes Protocol Stack Reset from Opal reset_types, so Done remains true and shadowing stays inactive; reading media pattern 77 is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: ProtocolStackReset is not one of the DoneOnReset reset_types shown in the evidence. Because Done was set true and only PowerCycle was listed, shadowing should remain inactive and LBA 0 should return media pattern 77, not MBR bytes AABBCCDD.

label: PASS
rationale: ProgrammaticResetEnable was set true and DoneOnReset contained 0 and 3. Opal says TPER_RESET sets MBRControl.Done false when DoneOnReset contains Programmatic, making shadowing active; the MBR writes produce 01AABB04, matching the read result.

label: FAIL
rationale: Because TPER_RESET was enabled and DoneOnReset included Programmatic, the reset should set Done=false and activate MBR shadowing. The MBR contents after the byte-table update are 01AABB04, so returning the media pattern 66 is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: DoneOnReset was set to {0} only. A TPER_RESET is Programmatic, and Opal only clears Done on TPER_RESET if DoneOnReset contains Programmatic, so Done remains true and the media pattern 55 is the compliant read result.

label: FAIL
rationale: The reset was Programmatic but DoneOnReset contained only PowerCycle. TPER_RESET therefore should not clear Done, leaving shadowing inactive and LBA 0 on the media pattern 55; returning MBR bytes DEADBEEF is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: Enable and Done were set true successfully, then the attempted DoneOnReset value {3} failed INVALID_PARAMETER. Since Programmatic-only is unsupported and does not mutate the previous DoneOnReset value, the default PowerCycle DoneOnReset remains; TPER_RESET does not clear Done, so reading media pattern 44 is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The successful MBRControl Set made Enable=true and Done=true, but the later DoneOnReset {3} Set failed INVALID_PARAMETER and does not replace the prior/default PowerCycle reset set. A Programmatic TPER_RESET therefore leaves Done true and shadowing inactive, so LBA 0 should return media pattern 44 rather than BEEFFEED.
